explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state, and the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then in the next state the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. It uses the template with index 11 (G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))).  bool_exp2 represents the condition for the transition, which is supported and sensor data is good (supported & good). bool_exp3 represents the desired state change, which is transitioning from PULLUP to TRANSITION (state_is_PULLUP & state_is_TRANSITION).

The LTL formula states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. It captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION under the specified conditions.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state. It uses a combination of the 'G' (always) and 'F' (eventually) temporal operators to express this. The boolean expressions ensure that the autopilot is only required to transition states when the system is supported and the sensor data is good.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. The property uses the implication operator to express this requirement. The antecedent of the implication is the condition of the system being supported and sensor data being good, represented by the boolean expression "supported & good". The consequent of the implication is the autopilot transitioning to the TRANSITION state in the next time step, represented by the boolean expression "X state_is_TRANSITION". The property ensures that this implication holds globally, meaning it must hold at every time step.

The LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state ('pullup' is true) and the system is supported ('supported' is true) and the sensor data is good ('good' is true), then in the next time step the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state ('state_is_TRANSITION' is true).

This LTL property ensures that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good (bool_exp2), the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3) in the next time step (X bool_exp3). It captures the requirement that the autopilot should switch from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state under these conditions.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state in the next time step.  The property uses a combination of atomic propositions and logical operators to define the conditions for state transition.

The LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION only when the system is healthy and the sensor data is reliable.  The LTL formula is a combination of a conjunction and a next operator, ensuring the change of state happens in the next time step.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state from the PULLUP state within a time frame of 1 time unit. This is achieved by using the "G" (always) operator to ensure the condition holds for all states, the "&" (and) operator to combine the system support and good sensor data conditions, the "->" (implies) operator to connect the conditions to the state change, the "X" (next) operator to indicate the immediate next state, and the "F" (eventually) operator to ensure the state change happens within the specified duration. The atomic proposition "state_is_PULLUP" is used to represent the initial state of the autopilot, and "state_is_TRANSITION" represents the target state.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. It captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION under these specific conditions.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good (bool_exp2) and the current autopilot state is PULLUP (bool_exp1), then in the next state the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot should change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION. It uses template 11, which specifies that whenever a condition (bool_exp2) holds, the next state (X) should satisfy a specific boolean expression (bool_exp3). In this case, bool_exp2 represents the conditions 'supported' and 'good', while bool_exp3 represents the transition from state PULLUP to TRANSITION, which is indicated by the conjunction of 'state_is_PULLUP' and 'state_is_TRANSITION'. This ensures that the autopilot will always transition from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the required conditions are met.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot is guaranteed to be in the TRANSITION state in the next time step if it was previously in the PULLUP state. The property uses the implication operator to express this relationship. The left side of the implication represents the precondition, which is the system being supported and sensor data being good, and the right side of the implication represents the postcondition, which is the autopilot being in the TRANSITION state in the next time step.

The G operator ensures that this property holds globally, meaning it must hold at all times. The implication "supported & good -> F state_is_TRANSITION" ensures that if both the supported and good conditions are true, then eventually the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state within a fixed duration of N_DURATION time steps. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the system is supported and sensor data is good, while bool_exp3 represents the state being PULLUP, and bool_exp4 represents the state being TRANSITION.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the next state of the autopilot must be TRANSITION, given that the current state is PULLUP. This is achieved using template 11, which expresses that whenever bool_exp2 is true, the next state must be bool_exp3. The boolean expression bool_exp2 is defined as supported & good & state_is_PULLUP, representing the conditions for the state change. The boolean expression bool_exp3 is defined as state_is_TRANSITION, representing the target state.

The LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within a bounded timeframe. The property uses the "G" (always) operator to ensure that the condition holds in all states. The "bool_exp2" represents the condition of the system being supported and sensor data being good. The "bool_exp3" represents the transition to the TRANSITION state. The "F[0,N_DURATION]" (eventually within N_DURATION steps) operator ensures that the transition occurs within the bounded timeframe. N_DURATION is set to 1, which means the transition must occur within one step.
